title: stellaria borealis subsp. bongardiana
intro: Stellaria borealis subsp. bongardiana, also known as Bongard's starry flax or Northern starflower, is a perennial plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Bongard's starry flax is often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat a range of ailments, from minor skin irritations to more serious conditions such as bronchitis.
